scope: VTS, port-control, pilot, tug, and traffic-authority communication for M3 corpus growth.

dangerous_minimal_pairs: VTS / port control / marina control / pilot station
qa_notes.linguist_note: VTS is the vessel traffic service station, not a pilot or marina station.

dangerous_minimal_pairs: VTS / port control / marina control / pilot station
qa_notes.linguist_note: Port Control remains a station identity, not a generic harbour service.
Instruction is binding authority language; advice and information stay separate.
Advice is a message noun; instruction and advise must stay wrong.
Traffic information is not a warning, weather report, or pilot information.
Pilot must stay separate from tug and station identity.
Tug is the assisting vessel, not the action tow or the pilot.
Clearance is an authority permission, not advice, information, or the request itself.
Anchorage, berth, fairway, and reporting point remain different places.
Fairway is the navigable channel area, not anchorage or berth.
Calling VTS is station contact, not pilot or marina contact.
Report and request must stay separate.
The request is not a report or the granted clearance.
Wait, proceed, leave, and outside area are different instructions.
Proceed to a point differs from reporting at it or stopping there.
Hold position is not a report, movement, or course instruction.
Listening watch is not stand by, go ahead, or stop transmitting.
Traffic clear and traffic dense are opposite status cues.
Traffic density is not visibility or generic information.
Pilot requirement differs from request and pilot-on-board state.
Pilot on board is a state, not a requirement.
Tug requirement must not collapse into pilot or tow wording.
Enter port is not leaving, reporting, or only entering the fairway.
Leave port differs from enter port and leaving berth.
Embark and disembark pilot are opposite actions.
Disembark pilot is not embark pilot or crew disembarkation.
Channel numbers must remain exact.

items.target_text: VTS, this is sailing yacht Aurora.
accepted_answers: vts this is sailing yacht aurora
vessel traffic service this is sailing yacht aurora

should_accept: VTS, this is sailing yacht Aurora.
should_reject: marina control this is sailing yacht aurora
pilot station this is sailing yacht aurora
dangerous_minimal_pairs: VTS / port control / marina control / pilot station
qa_notes.linguist_note: Station called first; VTS is not marina or pilot station.

items.source_text: Я нахожусь у точки доклада Alpha.
items.target_text: I am at reporting point Alpha.
accepted_answers: i am at reporting point alpha

should_accept: I am at reporting point Alpha.
should_reject: i am at reporting point bravo
i am passing reporting point alpha
qa_notes.linguist_note: At a point differs from passing it and from anchorage.

items.source_text: Я подхожу к точке доклада Bravo.
items.target_text: I am approaching reporting point Bravo.
accepted_answers: i am approaching reporting point bravo

should_accept: I am approaching reporting point Bravo.
should_reject: i am leaving reporting point bravo
i am approaching reporting point alpha
qa_notes.linguist_note: Reporting point name and movement verb must stay exact.
Entry request must not become a report or departure request.
Departure request must not become entry or berth movement.
Pilot request is not tug, berth, or report.
Pilot-on-board report stays distinct from requirement and tug state.
Tug assistance is not pilot or generic towing assistance.
Requirement and availability must stay separate.
Instruction destination must stay exact.
Hold position must not become report, proceed, or hold course.
Instruction, advice, request, and pilot source stay separate.

items.source_text: Информация о движении: судно пересекает курс впереди.

qa_notes.linguist_note: Traffic information is not warning and ahead/astern must stay exact.

items.source_text: Информация о движении: выходящее судно в фарватере.
items.target_text: Traffic information: outbound vessel in fairway.
accepted_answers: traffic information outbound vessel in fairway

should_accept: Traffic information: outbound vessel in fairway.
should_reject: traffic information inbound vessel in fairway
traffic information outbound vessel at anchorage
traffic information pilot vessel in fairway
qa_notes.linguist_note: Outbound/inbound, fairway/anchorage, and vessel type stay exact.
Keep clear instruction is not entry or traffic-clear status.
Negative entry instruction must stay exact.
Outside/inside/berth positions must stay distinct.

items.source_text: Сообщите, когда лоцман будет на борту.
accepted_answers: report when pilot is on board

should_accept: report when pilot is on board
qa_notes.linguist_note: Report trigger must remain pilot-on-board, not tug or request.

items.source_text: Сообщите при прохождении точки доклада Alpha.
items.target_text: Report when passing reporting point Alpha.
accepted_answers: report when passing reporting point alpha

should_accept: Report when passing reporting point Alpha.
should_reject: report when passing reporting point bravo
qa_notes.linguist_note: Point name and report/request boundary must stay exact.

switch to vessel traffic service channel 12

should_accept: switch to VTS channel one two
dangerous_minimal_pairs: VTS / port control / marina control / pilot station
qa_notes.linguist_note: Station identity and channel number must stay exact.

accepted_answers: stand by on vts channel one two

should_accept: stand by on VTS channel one two
qa_notes.linguist_note: Procedure word, station, and channel number must stay exact.
Permitted and not permitted are opposite authority results.
Not permitted must not collapse into permitted or a general closure notice.

grammar_patterns.pattern: authority station + this is + vessel identity
examples: VTS, this is sailing yacht Aurora.
I am at reporting point Alpha.
Report when passing reporting point Alpha.

grammar_patterns.pattern: request permission/clearance to enter or leave port
request or report pilot/tug service status
VTS movement instruction: proceed, hold, wait, keep clear

examples: Traffic information: outbound vessel in fairway.

grammar_patterns.pattern: procedure word + authority station + exact channel
pilot status report or report trigger

phrase_vts_report_passing_alpha_001: Report when passing reporting point Alpha.
report when passing reporting point bravo

dangerous_minimal_pairs.reason: Request asks for authority or service; report provides information.
Instruction, advice, and information have different authority weight.

dangerous_minimal_pairs.pair: VTS / port control / marina control / pilot station

phrase_vts_call_aurora_001: VTS, this is sailing yacht Aurora.
marina control this is sailing yacht aurora
pilot station this is sailing yacht aurora

dangerous_minimal_pairs.reason: Station identity must not collapse across traffic authority, marina, and pilot stations.
Pilotage and tug assistance are separate services.
Entry and departure instructions are opposite port movements.
Permission and refusal are opposite authority results.

phrase_vts_at_reporting_point_alpha_001: I am at reporting point Alpha.
i am at reporting point bravo
i am passing reporting point alpha

dangerous_minimal_pairs.reason: Port areas and reporting points are operationally different locations.
Proceed, hold, and wait are different movement instructions.

traffic information inbound vessel in fairway
traffic information outbound vessel at anchorage
traffic information pilot vessel in fairway

dangerous_minimal_pairs.reason: Inbound and outbound traffic directions must not be interchangeable.

producer_notes.status: linguist reviewed; not merged into starter.json
producer_notes.next_gate: Director-Engineer engineering gate before QA or playable merge
open_questions: Confirm whether VTS and Port Control should both be trained as station identities in this beginner/intermediate batch.
Confirm whether request clearance and request permission should both be accepted item-locally for port entry/departure.
Confirm pilot/tug/tow boundaries before regression expansion.
Confirm whether keep listening watch duplicates an existing urgency phrase acceptably inside VTS context.
Confirm whether port entry is not permitted should reject general port closed wording in this item.
risk_focus: VTS / port control / marina control / pilot station

decisions: Keep VTS and Port Control as separate trainable station identities; reject marina control and pilot station substitutions.
Keep request clearance and request permission as item-local accepted variants for port entry/departure requests only.
Keep pilot, tug, and tow boundaries strict; tug assistance is not pilot assistance or towing assistance in this batch.
Keep keep listening watch as a valid VTS-context expression while rejecting stand by, go ahead, and stop transmitting for that item.
Keep port closed rejected for the item Port entry is not permitted; the item trains permission/refusal wording, not a general closure notice.
Remove nonstandard accepted variant traffic information traffic crossing ahead from the vessel-crossing traffic-information item.

content_patches.reason: Nonstandard duplicated wording; canonical vessel-crossing wording remains accepted.
